title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During their meeting in Hertfordshire, Agnes Fletcher reveals to Hercule Poirot that she and the other cooks initially believed Mr. Morley’s death was a suicide caused by a medicinal error, as reported in the newspapers. However, Agnes expresses unease following news reports that Frank Carter, Miss Neville’s boyfriend, had shot a man and appeared to be losing his mind. Agnes recalls that on the morning of Mr. Morley's death, around 12:30, she saw Frank Carter standing on the stairs above the owner's office, appearing to strain to hear something. She then witnessed him run down the stairs and into the owner's consulting room. Agnes and her colleague Emma had previously withheld this information to avoid causing trouble for Frank, but Agnes now suspects he may have killed the owner.

Poirot visits Inspector Japp at Scotland Yard to request a meeting with Frank Carter. Japp suggests Poirot is acting out of sentimentality for Frank or his girlfriend, but Poirot denies this, asserting his rationality. Poirot informs Japp that he will soon provide the name and address of a witness who can testify that Frank committed the crime, though he insists on seeing Frank personally to satisfy himself.

Poirot subsequently meets with Frank Carter, who appears pale and haggard. Frank reacts with scorn and hostility toward Poirot, refusing to speak without his lawyer present. He accuses Poirot of attempting to set a trap to force a confession and suspects that the police are eavesdropping on their conversation.
